name: What kind of ongoing maintenance does exterior siding need in a coastal climate?
headline: What kind of ongoing maintenance does exterior siding need in a coastal climate?

mainEntity.name: What kind of ongoing maintenance does exterior siding need in a coastal climate?
mainEntity.text: What kind of ongoing maintenance does exterior siding need in a coastal climate?

acceptedAnswer.text: In places with frequent fog and salt in the air, you should inspect your siding at least **once a year**, and do small maintenance tasks as needed. Simple yearly checks include: - Look for peeling paint, cracked caulk, or open joints around windows, doors, and trim. - Check the bottom edges of siding near decks, roofs, and soil for swelling, rot, or insect damage. - Make sure plants, soil, and sprinklers are not constantly wetting the siding. - Clean off heavy dirt, algae, or mildew with gentle washing if needed. Painted wood or fiber‑cement siding typically needs repainting every 7–15 years, depending on product quality, color, and exposure. Dark colors in sunny spots can age faster. On the foggy coast, moisture is the main enemy; for inner Bay Area neighborhoods, UV and heat matter more. Pitfalls: - Power‑washing too aggressively and forcing water behind the siding. - Painting over dirty or peeling surfaces without proper prep. - Ignoring small cracks and gaps that slowly let in water. A bit of routine maintenance greatly extends siding life and prevents big repairs. Keep a simple record of when you last painted, recaulked, or repaired areas so you can plan future work instead of waiting for problems.
